name: Odstock
overview: Odstock is a village and civil parish about 3 miles south of Salisbury in Wiltshire, with a population of around 554. The parish also includes the village of Nunton and the hamlet of Bodenham. It sits in beautiful countryside at the meeting of the Avon and Ebble valleys, and gives its name to the original Odstock Hospital (built 1942 just to the north in Britford parish), which grew into the major Salisbury District Hospital with its renowned burns and spinal units.
character: A quiet, attractive downland-and-valley village close to Salisbury, valued for its rural calm, woodland walks and proximity to the city and hospital.
amenities: The Yew Tree Inn (village pub in former 18th-century cottages)
Salisbury District Hospital nearby (~1 mile north)
Avon Valley and Ebble Valley walking paths intersect here
Farm shop and pub in neighbouring Nunton

schools.name: Primary schools in Salisbury / nearby Coombe Bissett

prices.summary: A sought-after village close to Salisbury and its hospital; period cottages and family homes in a quiet valley setting. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: Walks in Odstock woods (bluebells in spring)
The Avon Valley and Ebble Valley paths
placesToEat: Pub and farm shop in nearby Nunton
pros: Quiet valley setting only 3 miles from Salisbury
Village pub and easy access to the District Hospital
Excellent walking on the Avon and Ebble valley paths
cons: No village shop or school within Odstock itself
No railway station; rely on Salisbury
Hospital traffic on nearby roads at peak times
whoItSuits: Buyers (including hospital staff) wanting a quiet valley village within a short drive or bus ride of Salisbury.
primarySupermarket: Farm shop in Nunton; supermarkets in Salisbury

sources.label: Hillside Self Catering - Odstock village setting
